target.title: fix(ci): include runtime resources in build artifact
target.body:
﻿Closes #98276

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users downloading the CI `dist-runtime-build` artifact for gateway or ACP runtime proof would need local repairs before the artifact could resolve workspace templates and plugin runtime modules.

## Why This Change Was Made

The runtime artifact now preserves the package-root launcher, package metadata, docs workspace templates, runtime agent templates, and built `dist`/`dist-runtime` outputs in the same archive. This keeps the existing package-root resolvers for `src/agents/templates`, `docs/reference/templates`, and `dist/plugins/runtime` working from the extracted artifact instead of requiring local copies or symlink repairs.

## User Impact

Developers and proof workflows can use the CI runtime artifact as a pinned runtime input with the packaged template and plugin runtime layout present. This does not claim the downstream evaOS visible-agent ACP behavior is fixed; it only fixes the artifact reproducibility gap tracked here.

## Evidence

- Claim proved: the CI workflow now packages the runtime artifact with package-root runtime resources needed by workspace template and plugin runtime resolution.
- Commands / artifacts:
  - `git diff --check` passed.
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/ci-workflow-guards.test.ts` passed: 32 tests.
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/workspace-templates.test.ts` passed: 5 tests.
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/openclaw-npm-release-check.test.ts` passed: 69 tests.
  - `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` passed with no actionable output.
- Observed result: the workflow guard asserts `dist-runtime-build.tar.zst` is packed from `openclaw.mjs`, `package.json`, `docs/reference/templates`, `src/agents/templates`, `dist`, and `dist-runtime`.
- Not tested / proof gaps: I did not run a full CI artifact build/extract gateway or ACP smoke locally; PR CI owns the full workflow artifact production path.
